response.justification: Strong software architect with 20 years of experience and proven expertise in distributed systems, security practices, and mentorship, but critically underqualified in the core requirement: deep C++ development. Only six months of dedicated professional C++ experience (National Instruments, 2014-2015), with current role focused on TypeScript/Node.js/AI infrastructure. Completely lacks industrial automation domain knowledge: no experience with MQTT, message brokers, industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC standards), or IoT development. Could theoretically ramp up given strong fundamentals and polyglot background, but represents significant hiring risk versus specialized C++ candidates with industrial systems experience.
strengths: Proven ability to architect and lead large-scale distributed systems, including low-latency microsservices handling millions of requests per second, with strong mentorship track record
Deep commitment to software craftsmanship, secure coding practices (TDD, quality standards), and technical documentation—foundational mindset for SDL and IEC 62443 compliance
Demonstrated polyglot flexibility and rapid technology adoption across 20-year career spanning web, embedded, hardware, and infrastructure domains
concerns: Severe C++ expertise gap: only ~6 months dedicated professional C++ work in 2014-2015; current role (2025-present) is TypeScript/Node.js, creating a 10+ year gap since last substantive C++ development
Zero demonstrated experience with industrial automation technologies and protocols (MQTT, message brokers, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, OPC UA) that are core to role responsibilities
Career trajectory is web/backend services focused; lacks hands-on embedded systems, hardware integration, or on-premises industrial solutions experience typical of automation machinery manufacturing
